name: Major Professional Sports Leagues
description: Trophies, records, formats, and champions of the NFL, MLB, NBA, NHL, and the Premier League

questions.question: What trophy is awarded to the NFL Super Bowl winner?
questions.explanation: The trophy was renamed in 1970 after legendary Green Bay Packers coach Vince Lombardi, who won the first two Super Bowls. Each trophy is handcrafted by Tiffany & Co. and is worth about $50,000 in raw materials alone.

questions.question: Which NFL team has won the most Super Bowls?
questions.explanation: The Patriots and the Pittsburgh Steelers are tied with 6 Super Bowl victories each. The Patriots won all 6 of theirs with Tom Brady at quarterback between 2001 and 2018.

questions.question: Which NFL team completed the only perfect season in the Super Bowl era, going 17-0 in 1972?
questions.explanation: The 1972 Dolphins remain the only team to finish a season with zero losses, including the playoffs and Super Bowl. Surviving members of that team famously toast with champagne each year when the last undefeated team loses.

questions.question: How many teams are in the NFL?
questions.explanation: The NFL has 32 teams split into two conferences (AFC and NFC) of 16 teams each. The Houston Texans, who joined in 2002, were the last expansion team added.

questions.question: Which team won Super Bowl LVIII in February 2024?
questions.explanation: The Chiefs defeated the San Francisco 49ers 25-22 in overtime in Las Vegas, giving Patrick Mahomes his third Super Bowl title. Kansas City became the first team to win back-to-back Super Bowls since the 2003-2004 Patriots.

questions.question: What trophy is awarded to the World Series winner in Major League Baseball?
questions.explanation: Unlike the Stanley Cup or Lombardi Trophy, a brand-new Commissioner's Trophy is made each year. It features flags representing each MLB team and is the only major North American sports trophy that is not named after a person.

questions.question: Which MLB team has won the most World Series titles?
questions.explanation: The Yankees have won 27 World Series championships, nearly three times more than the next closest team. Their dynasty years from 1947 to 1962 alone produced 10 titles.

questions.question: Who holds the MLB record for most home runs in a single season with 73?
questions.explanation: Bonds hit 73 home runs in 2001, breaking Mark McGwire's record of 70 set just three years earlier. The record remains controversial because of Bonds' connection to performance-enhancing drugs, and he has not been inducted into the Hall of Fame by the baseball writers.

questions.question: Who holds the MLB record for most consecutive games played with 2,632?
questions.explanation: Ripken broke Lou Gehrig's seemingly unbreakable streak of 2,130 games on September 6, 1995, in one of baseball's most celebrated moments. His streak stretched from 1982 to 1998 -- over 16 years without missing a single game.

questions.question: Which team won the 2024 World Series?
questions.explanation: The Dodgers defeated the New York Yankees in five games to win their second World Series title in five years. Shohei Ohtani, in his first season with Los Angeles, reached the Fall Classic in his first trip to the postseason.

questions.question: What trophy is awarded to the NBA Finals winner?
questions.explanation: The trophy was renamed in 1984 after former NBA Commissioner Larry O'Brien, who helped secure the league's landmark television deals. Before that, it was simply called the Walter A. Brown Trophy.

questions.question: Which NBA franchise has won the most championships?
questions.explanation: The Celtics have won 18 NBA championships, including an incredible run of 11 titles in 13 seasons from 1957 to 1969 led by Bill Russell. They claimed their most recent title in 2024.

questions.question: Who is the NBA's all-time leading scorer?
questions.explanation: LeBron James passed Kareem Abdul-Jabbar's 38,387-point record on February 7, 2023. Abdul-Jabbar had held the record for nearly 39 years, a span so long that many assumed it would never be broken.

questions.question: Which team won the 2024 NBA Finals?
questions.explanation: The Celtics defeated the Dallas Mavericks 4 games to 1, claiming their 18th championship and breaking a tie with the Los Angeles Lakers for the most titles in NBA history. Jaylen Brown won Finals MVP.

questions.question: Which player scored 100 points in a single NBA game on March 2, 1962?
questions.explanation: Chamberlain scored 100 points for the Philadelphia Warriors against the New York Knicks. No photograph or video of the feat exists because the game was played in Hershey, Pennsylvania and was not televised. Only about 4,000 fans were there to witness it.

questions.question: What is the name of the trophy awarded to the NHL playoff champion?
questions.explanation: The Stanley Cup, donated by Lord Stanley of Preston in 1893, is the oldest professional sports trophy in North America. Unlike other major trophies, there is only one Stanley Cup -- it is not remade each year. Each winning team gets its players' names engraved on it.

questions.question: Which NHL team has won the most Stanley Cup championships?
questions.explanation: The Canadiens have won 24 Stanley Cups, far more than any other team. Their dynasty in the 1950s-70s was so dominant that they won 10 Cups in 15 years. However, their last championship was in 1993.

questions.question: Who holds the NHL record for most career goals with 894?
questions.explanation: Gretzky's record is so untouchable that even if you took away every one of his 894 goals, he would still be the NHL's all-time points leader on assists alone. He finished with 2,857 career points -- nearly 1,000 more than the second-place player.

questions.question: Which team won the 2024 Stanley Cup?
questions.explanation: The Panthers defeated the Edmonton Oilers in seven games, winning the franchise's first Stanley Cup. Edmonton had rallied from a 3-0 series deficit to force a decisive Game 7, but Florida won the clincher 2-1.

questions.question: What is the name of the top-tier professional football league in England?
questions.explanation: The Premier League was founded in 1992 when the top 22 clubs broke away from the Football League to negotiate their own television rights. It has since become the most-watched sports league in the world, broadcast to over 200 countries.

questions.question: Which club has won the most English Premier League titles?
questions.explanation: Manchester United has won 13 Premier League titles (20 total English top-flight titles), with Sir Alex Ferguson managing the club for all 13. Since Ferguson retired in 2013, the club has not won the league once.

questions.question: Which club shocked the world by winning the Premier League in 2015-16 despite 5000-to-1 odds?
questions.explanation: Leicester City had narrowly avoided relegation the year before, making their title win one of the greatest upsets in all of sports history. Their star striker Jamie Vardy set a record by scoring in 11 consecutive Premier League matches that season.

questions.question: How many teams are relegated from the Premier League at the end of each season?
questions.explanation: The bottom 3 teams are relegated to the Championship (second tier) and replaced by promoted clubs. This system, foreign to American sports, means every season is high-stakes even for mid-table teams -- one bad run can mean financial disaster.

questions.question: Who holds the record for most goals in a single Premier League season with 34?
questions.explanation: Haaland smashed the record in his debut Premier League season in 2022-23, breaking the previous mark of 32 held jointly by Alan Shearer and Andy Cole. He scored 36 total in all competitions for Manchester City that season.

questions.question: Which of the major North American leagues -- NFL, MLB, NBA, or NHL -- does NOT have a salary cap?
questions.explanation: MLB uses a luxury tax (officially the Competitive Balance Tax) instead of a hard cap. Teams can spend as much as they want but pay escalating penalties above a threshold, which is why the Yankees and Dodgers can consistently outspend smaller-market teams.
